content: Just as language learners use written reflexion (spelled the traditional English way) in high school writing workshops to correct repeated grammatical errors by reviewing their own past outputs, large language models can leverage LLM self-reflection mechanisms to correct persistent reasoning mistakes by grounding reflection in structured, human-style learning reflexion logs. This framing turns LLM self-reflection from a niche AI architecture tweak into a formalized learning practice mirrored in how humans intentionally build meta-cognitive skill.
